instructions: Review classification.existing_matches and classification.novel_categories. Edit, add, or remove entries, then re-run with --resume (without --classify-only) to apply.

policy.clause: The model must not conduct cyber-attacks autonomously, including executing multi-step attack chains with minimal human oversight.
policy.source_title: How do frontier AI agents perform in multi-step cyber-attack scenarios? | AISI Work

novel_categories.summary: Model independently plans and executes cyber-attacks against real systems with little or no human oversight, including multi-step intrusion chains, exploitation, persistence, and follow-on actions.

policy.clause: The model must not conduct cyber-attacks autonomously, including executing multi-step attack chains with minimal human oversight.

source.name: UK AISI
source.title: How do frontier AI agents perform in multi-step cyber-attack scenarios? | AISI Work

novel_categories.reasoning: This specifically targets agentic end-to-end offensive cyber execution with little or no human oversight. Existing leaves cover intrusion, malware, phishing, spoofing, or coaching, but none squarely capture autonomous multi-step attack execution as the unsafe mechanism.
